scenario.title: The Multi-Source Aggregator
scenario.context: Your data pipeline receives customer information from multiple sources (CRM export, email signatures, LinkedIn). The AI needs to merge these into a single canonical record, handling conflicts and deduplication.
scenario.goal: Write a prompt that merges data from multiple sources, resolving conflicts with priority rules and identifying duplicates.
constraints: Define source priority: CRM > LinkedIn > Email signature
Handle conflicting data (different phone numbers, titles)
Track data provenance (which source each field came from)
scenario.exampleInput: Source 1 (CRM): {name: 'Sarah Chen', email: 's.chen@techcorp.com', phone: '555-1234', title: 'VP Engineering'}\nSource 2 (LinkedIn): {name: 'Sarah Chen', title: 'VP of Engineering', company: 'TechCorp', location: 'San Francisco'}\nSource 3 (Email sig): {name: 'Sarah', email: 's.chen@techcorp.com', phone: '555-5678', title: 'VP Eng'}
hints: Define merge strategy for each field type

rubric.name: Source Priority
rubric.description: Are source priority rules clearly defined?

scoringGuide.excellent: Clear priority hierarchy with rationale and per-field rules
Full provenance per field with confidence

evaluatorContext.idealResponse:
Merge these data sources into a single canonical record.

Source priority (highest to lowest):
1. CRM - authoritative for contact details
2. LinkedIn - authoritative for job title/company
3. Email signature - supplementary only

Output schema:
```json
{
  "canonical": {
    "name": "Sarah Chen",
    "email": "s.chen@techcorp.com",
    "phone": "5551234",
    "title": "VP of Engineering",
    "company": "TechCorp",
    "location": "San Francisco"
  },
  "provenance": {
    "name": {"value": "Sarah Chen", "source": "crm", "confidence": "high"},
    "email": {"value": "s.chen@techcorp.com", "source": "crm", "confidence": "high"},
    "phone": {"value": "5551234", "source": "crm", "confidence": "high", "conflict": "email_sig:5555678"},
    "title": {"value": "VP of Engineering", "source": "linkedin", "confidence": "high"},
    "company": {"value": "TechCorp", "source": "linkedin", "confidence": "high"},
    "location": {"value": "San Francisco", "source": "linkedin", "confidence": "medium"}
  },
  "conflicts": [
    {
      "field": "phone",
      "values": [{"source": "crm", "value": "555-1234"}, {"source": "email_sig", "value": "555-5678"}],
      "resolution": "Used CRM value (higher priority)"
    },
    {
      "field": "title",
      "values": [{"source": "crm", "value": "VP Engineering"}, {"source": "linkedin", "value": "VP of Engineering"}],
      "resolution": "Used LinkedIn value (more complete)"
    }
  ],
  "match_confidence": "high",
  "match_reason": "Email exact match across sources"
}
```

Merge rules:
- name: Use most complete version from highest priority source
- email: Highest priority source, normalize to lowercase
- phone: Highest priority source, flag if conflict exists
- title: Use most complete version (prefer 'VP of Engineering' over 'VP Eng')
- For missing fields: use lower priority source if available

Match rules for duplicate detection:
- Exact email match → high confidence
- Name + company match → medium confidence
- Partial name + same domain → low confidence

Return ONLY the JSON.
